verdict: The Eid Al-Adha Awareness push on Instagram is generating attention but very few sales. Over the last 28 days, the account delivered roughly 769 landing page views and only 7 add-to-cart actions, with 1 checkout started and a recorded purchase value of just $14. With Eid Al-Adha demand peaking in the next several days, the priority is converting the awareness you've already built into actual orders — not adding more reach.
diagnosis: The "Eid Qurbani Without the Hassle" Instagram campaign is doing the top of the funnel job well: 1,317 reach entries logged, 1,526 unique clicks, and engagement on the new Picanha Kabobs creative reached 489 in the latest period — a sharp jump versus the 221 average. However, the funnel collapses between interest and purchase. Only 7 add-to-cart events and 1 initiated checkout were recorded in this period, and the cost per purchase landed at $204 on average (latest period $155). For a category where industry research pegs typical customer acquisition for food brands at $15–$35, this is roughly 5–10x what a DTC food brand should be paying to land a customer. The Google Business Profile is also losing signal: 0 new reviews, 0 calls, and 0 direction requests recorded this period despite a base of 419 lifetime reviews — meaning the local discovery channel that usually supports a butcher shop ("zabiha halal meat near me" is literally one of your tracked terms) is dormant heading into the single biggest holiday of your year.
